title: Free the States Statement on Sen. Jason Smalley's Resignation
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States responds to Oklahoma State Senator Jason Smalley's January 2020 resignation, framing his departure as evidence of a paradigm shift in which Oklahomans are no longer willing to give 'pro-life' politicians a pass when they block immediate-abolition legislation like SB13. The piece recounts the abolitionist accountability campaign run against Smalley after he refused to hear the bill in 2019 and calls for his replacement to grant SB13 a hearing.

questions.question: What specific procedural action by Smalley made him a target of the abolitionist movement?
questions.answer: As chairman of the Senate Health and Human Services Committee, Smalley refused to schedule a hearing for the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) during the 2019 session, killing it in committee without a vote. The bill was left sitting in the same committee going into 2020.
questions.quote: Smalley, who was chairman of the Senate Health and Human Services Committee, refused to give the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) a hearing in 2019. The bill remains in the same committee for the upcoming session.

questions.question: Why does Free the States argue that the political environment for blocking abolition bills has changed?
questions.answer: Silberman argues that prior Senate leaders could quietly kill the bill from 2016-2018 without consequence, but a 'paradigm shift' has made that strategy untenable. Smalley is presented as a politician who underestimated this shift and learned its magnitude through the public response to his obstruction.
questions.quote: There was a time when a pro-life State Senator could get away with preventing the abolition of abortion. Senate leaders were able to kill the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act quietly in 2016-18. But the paradigm is shifting.

questions.question: What grassroots tactics did abolitionists use to publicize Smalley's obstruction of SB13?
questions.answer: Abolitionists from Smalley's district and statewide canvassed Stroud and surrounding areas with fliers explaining what he had done, and put up billboards around Oklahoma City carrying the same message. The effort tied his name directly to keeping abortion legal.
questions.quote: When Smalley refused to hear SB13 in 2019, abolitionists from Smalley's district and around the state canvassed Stroud and the surrounding areas with fliers explaining what Smalley had done. We put up billboards around Oklahoma City communicating the same message.

questions.question: How does the article reframe what a '100 percent pro-life' legislative record actually means in practice?
questions.answer: The article inverts the conventional reading of pro-life ratings, claiming that Republicans with perfect pro-life scorecards are precisely the ones obstructing abolition. Pro-life branding, in this account, often signals the opposite of action toward ending abortion.
questions.quote: The people of Oklahoma are waking up to the fact that when a politician says he or she is pro-life, that doesn't mean they're going to do anything to abolish abortion. In fact, it often means the opposite. It's Republicans with 100 percent pro-life records who are delaying abortion's abolition.

questions.question: What evidence does Silberman cite to support the claim that a paradigm shift is underway in Oklahoma?
questions.answer: He points to two Christian denominations (the Oklahoma Free Will Baptists and the Oklahoma Baptists) passing immediate-abolition resolutions, the Oklahoma Republican Party calling for SB13 floor votes, pastors preaching immediate abolition from the pulpit, and thousands of Oklahomans converting from pro-life to abolitionist positions.
questions.quote: In the past year, two Christian denominations (Oklahoma Free Will Baptists and the Oklahoma Baptists) have passed resolutions calling for abortion's immediate abolition and a rejection of compromise. The Republican party of Oklahoma passed a resolution calling for SB13 to be brought to the floor of both chambers for a vote. Pastors all over the state are preaching immediate abolition from the pulpit. Thousands of Oklahomans in every corner of the state are converting from the pro-life movement to the abolitionist movement.

questions.question: How does Free the States justify its public exposure tactics against individual politicians?
questions.answer: Silberman frames exposure as a reluctant but necessary instrument: the organization claims it does not want to publicly shame politicians or lobbyists, but accountability is unavoidable when the goal is stopping the killing of preborn children.
questions.quote: The last thing in the world the abolitionists of Oklahoma want to do is expose politicians and lobbyists for keeping abortion legal. We just want the killing to stop. But that means holding people accountable.

questions.question: What is the explicit ask Free the States makes regarding Smalley's replacement on the committee?
questions.answer: Free the States calls on the new Health and Human Services Committee member who replaces Smalley to grant SB13 a hearing and vote in favor of it, treating equal protection of preborn human beings as the standard. The group commits to making sure the replacement understands this is what Oklahomans demand.
questions.quote: It's our prayer that Smalley's replacement on the Health and Human Services Committee will extend equal protection to all human beings by hearing and voting in favor of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act. We will work hard to make sure Smalley's replacement knows that that's exactly what the people of Oklahoma demand.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Earlier Silberman piece naming Smalley as one of the central pro-life politicians blocking SB13, providing the backstory the resignation article assumes.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Companion accountability piece on the Senate leadership figure who, alongside Smalley, kept SB13 from advancing.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Direct sequel: Smalley's replacement on the Health and Human Services Committee, McCortney, refuses the hearing the resignation statement called for.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Immediate Abolition Resolution
related.reason: The article hyperlinks this exact piece as evidence of the denominational paradigm shift it cites.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Published a week later in the same SB13 cycle, addressing the pro-life opposition tactics referenced in the resignation statement's argument.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13"?
related.reason: Long-form explanation of why Free the States insists on SB13 specifically, the bill Smalley refused to hear.

related.title: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Documents the broader pro-life lobby strategy to suppress SB13 that Smalley operationalized in committee.
